compositional_truth_check.p1_yellow_pot: Present, but appears largely dormant or empty of significant foliage.
compositional_truth_check.p2_black_pot: Systemic Loss; no visible Mexican Mint foliage detected.
compositional_truth_check.p3_black_pot_with_rabbit: Present; contains a single Pothos leaf.
compositional_truth_check.p4_black_pot_shared: Systemic Loss; no visible Silver Guest foliage detected.
compositional_truth_check.new_introductions: Presence of eggshell fragments in the central black pot (P3/P4 area) acting as a soil amendment or debris.

inventory_reconciliation.status: Partial failure of registry. Only P3 remains active. P1, P2, and P4 show no viable plant matter.

plant_audit.p1: Yellow pot appears empty; soil surface is dark and undisturbed.
plant_audit.p2: No evidence of Mexican Mint; pot appears to be empty or containing only substrate.
plant_audit.p3: Single Pothos leaf remains stable. White rabbit anchor is present.
plant_audit.p4: No evidence of Silver Guest; pot appears to be empty or containing only substrate.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Consistently damp/dark across all pots; no signs of surface cracking.
biome_observations.debris: Eggshell fragments observed in the central pot, likely an intentional intervention.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_current: The Pothos leaf in P3 has maintained a consistent posture. No new growth or senescence observed in the Pothos. The other pots have remained static and devoid of visible plant life throughout the 5-day observation window.

visual_health_inference.p3_pothos: Stable/Static. The leaf shows no signs of chlorosis or necrosis.

anomalies: High rate of systemic loss across the registry.
vision_report.narrative_description: The biome is currently in a state of stasis. The only surviving specimen is the Pothos in the pot with the white rabbit anchor. The other pots (Yellow P1, and the secondary black pots) are effectively empty of visible plant life. The environment is stable, with no changes in soil moisture or plant posture over the last five days. The eggshell fragments suggest an attempt at soil enrichment, but this has not resulted in any new growth.
